spec_match.rationale: Builds a fully-shaped Attribution-Reporting-Register-Trigger registration JSON (aggregatable_trigger_data, aggregatable_values, trigger_data, trigger_context_id) and POSTs to the local backend route which emits the real response header. Pre/post cap behaviour is correct.

interactive.rationale: Six bound input fields drive a live JSON re-render, a header preview, and a backend-fetched bar comparison. Two-button toggle for ctx_id on/off — clearly the canonical builder concept for the feature.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a registration builder with sent-vs-dropped bar comparison flipping between modes; page delivers all of that plus the JSON + Header output blocks.

edge_cases.rationale: Numeric inputs are clamped (conv 1-200, trigData 0-7), and the catch surfaces backend errors. But the builder doesn't validate hex format on keyPiece (accepts any string), doesn't enforce ctxId character set (spec restricts to ASCII), and doesn't show the source-event-id length cap.

references.rationale: The page now includes a see-also section with the feature index, the trigger_context_id spec anchor, and chromestatus.com/feature/5079048977645568.

design_system.rationale: Variables used throughout, verdict pills override both background and colour together, primary button uses --text-black / --bg-ivory. No static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Validate keyPiece as hex
openQuestions.detail: The keyPiece input accepts any string but the spec requires a hex value like '0x000...'. Add a pattern attribute (pattern="0x[0-9a-fA-F]+") and surface a 'invalid key_piece' badge so the builder catches a common copy-paste mistake. Right now an invalid key just lands in the JSON and the backend probably 4xxs without a clear UI signal.

openQuestions.title: Enforce trigger_context_id character set + max length
openQuestions.detail: Spec restricts trigger_context_id to a 64-char ASCII string. The current input accepts arbitrary unicode and arbitrary length. Add a maxlength=64 and a pattern that rejects non-ASCII, then surface 'spec-valid' / 'spec-invalid' pills alongside the field so the constraint is part of the lesson.

openQuestions.title: Reveal the source-side counterpart
openQuestions.detail: The cap is per-source — but the source-event-id field is only used as a label here. Add a sibling input pair labelled 'source registration' that shows the Attribution-Reporting-Register-Source header that would have to be served from the publisher origin, so the demo shows both halves of the loop.

summary: Best builder in the feature and the only one that surfaces the actual response header text. The reference invariant is now satisfied; remaining follow-up is proper input validation for the keyPiece and trigger_context_id format constraints the spec demands.
